items.title: Watch: Explained: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India
items.description: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

items.title: Ukraine must 'get the support it needs': allies prepare for NATO summit
items.description: "Allies must ensure that Ukraine receives the support it needs in its war with Russia" according to NATO chief Mark Rutte, ahead of a summit set to take place in Ankara, Turkey. He has also said allies will be announcing contracts worth tens of billions to "deter and defend". FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us what to expect from the summit, and what it means for Ukraine.

items.title: Genneia files for first Argentine IPO in the United States since 2019
items.description: Power producer Genneia SA files for IPO in United States, the first Argentine firm to do so in at least seven years. Leer más

items.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
items.description: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

items.title: Is this a breakthrough moment for soccer in the US?
items.description: Victory for the national team against Belgium on Monday could mean the sport takes further strides among American audiences.

items.title: FIFA defends Balogun red card referee after Trump calls him ‘a bit suspect’
items.description: Raphael Claus is 'one of the world's leading professional referees', FIFA says, following Trump's comments.

items.title: Argentina pushes US$6 billion in repo maturities past election
items.description: Central Bank extends maturities worth US$6 billion on its repurchase agreements to ease the government debt burden. Leer más

items.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
items.description: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

items.title: Frenchman convicted in missing body case confesses to killing his wife, lawyer says
items.description: Cédric Jubillar, a painter and plasterer who was sentenced last October for killing his wife Delphine, has finally confessed to the murder from his prison cell, his lawyer said Monday. The 38-year-old said he was ready to tell investigators the location of his wife's body, which was never found.

items.title: Thousands evacuated from homes in southwest France as wildfire burns
items.description: The fire has scorched some 4,600 hectares ⁠in the foothills of the French Pyrenees, local prefect Pierre Regnault de la Mothe said in a post on X

items.title: Messi returns 'home' to lead Argentina World Cup charge in Miami
items.description: Argentina's skipper returns to the Florida city he now calls home for a last 32 clash against the surprise package of the tournament: Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
items.description: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

items.title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants in visit to island frontier
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has travelled to the Italian island of Lampedusa, one of Europe's main gateways for migrants crossing the Mediterranean. In a powerful appeal, the pontiff urged Europe to do more to protect and integrate those fleeing conflict and poverty.

items.title: Bandit raid leaves nine farmers dead in northwest Nigeria
items.description: At least nine farmers have been killed and several others abducted in a deadly raid on a farming community in Nigeria's Kaduna State, underscoring the growing insecurity threatening rural livelihoods as armed gangs intensify attacks during the country's planting season.

items.title: OPEC+ raises output but cartel's future hangs in balance
items.description: A handful of OPEC+ countries have agreed to increase their output targets from August but the cartel is fighting for its survival after the UAE's departure. A French company is using the Seine to cool down Paris' buildings. And, Sky says it's buying ITV's broadcast channels and streaming service for £1.6bn.

items.title: Canada awards Germany major submarine contract
items.description: Canadian PM Carney said the deal of up to 12 German-built submarines will be the "largest in Canadian history." Germany had pushed hard for the order, which comes as Canada seeks stronger ties with Europe.

items.title: Israeli drone strike kills four people in southern Lebanon, Lebanese state media reports
items.description: The intensity ⁠of hostilities abated last month, as Iran insisted on its ‌demand for a Lebanon ceasefire in talks with the U.S., and as Israel scaled back attacks in Lebanon at Washington’s ‌behest

items.title: Lionel Scaloni admits Argentina escape after Cape Verde scare
items.description: Argentina's coach says defeat to Cape Verde would have been "madness"; World champions survived extra-time thriller to reach the World Cup last 16, where they will face Egypt. Leer más

items.description: The latest report by the UN independent commission of inquiry captures Israel’s crimes against Palestinian children

items.title: Belgian coach says his comments were not a criticism of African football
items.description: Rudi Garcia said he was referring to teams unused to dealing with the kind of pressure that comes with managing a lead in high-level World Cup game

items.description: Africa Today: DR Congo boosts Ebola care, Tanzania deploys security ahead of protests

items.title: Online bookmaker Betr shifts operations from NT to Tasmania
items.description: With sports bookmaker Betr granted a licence to operate in Tasmania, independent MP Andrew Wilkie slams the government for "rolling out the red carpet" despite the state admitting its regulations are not up to scratch.

items.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
items.description: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

items.title: Pumas ready to take on rugby's heavyweights in Nations Championship
items.description: Pumas, ranked fifth in the latest World Rugby rankings, to take on sport's leading nations in the Nations Championship, which starts this weekend. Leer más

items.title: Girl trapped in Venezuela quake 'survived on ketchup and cheese'
items.description: BBC international correspondent Yogita Limayee meets 12-year-old Fabiana, who was trapped in Venezuela's devastating earthquakes.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Zelensky to press Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
items.description: The Ukrainian president will use the Nato meeting in Turkey to argue he needs more interceptor missiles.

items.title: Milei star guest at US Embassy’s 250th independence day celebration
items.description: Javier Milei first sitting Argentine president to attend US Embassy’s annual party in Buenos Aires; Ambassador Peter Lamelas hails relationship between two nations as United States marks 250 years of US independence. Leer más

items.title: British jets intercept Russian aircraft
items.description: “The Russian aircraft was intercepted and escorted by two U.K. F-35 jets from HMS Prince of Wales until it left the area,” the statement said

items.title: Pope leaves for summer holiday after whirlwind start to 2026
items.description: Pope Leo XIV began a summer vacation on Sunday, capping a whirlwind finale to the first half of 2026 in which he emerged as a powerful global statesman on issues from artificial intelligence to war and flexed decisive papal muscle to govern internal church problems.

items.title: Next round of U.S.-Iran talks likely to be held at Islamabad: report
items.description: The technical talks are expected to take place on July 11, although a final decision on the venue has yet to be announced

items.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
items.description: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: SEC lifts ban on BGL Securities, BGL Asset Management
items.description: “Suspension is lifted from BGL Securities and BGL Asset Management,” the SEC confirmed in its response to PREMIUM TIMES’ media inquiry into the registration status of the two entities. The post SEC lifts ban on BGL Securities, BGL Asset Management appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Queiroz steps down after Ghana's World Cup disappointment
items.description: Carlos Queiroz has resigned as Ghana head coach after the Black Stars' World Cup campaign ended with a 1-0 defeat to Colombia in the last 16. The Portuguese coach said he leaves with pride but urged reforms off the pitch to secure the team's future success.

items.title: U.K.'s Farage referred to standards watchdog after new report of undeclared benefits
items.description: Mr. Farage’s spokesperson said ⁠the story was “baseless and contrived”

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.

items.title: Russia's missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 22
items.description: Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people. The attacks exposed gaps in the country's air defenses more than four years into Moscow's full-scale invasion.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Hamas to dissolve Gaza governing body
items.description: Officials for the militant organization have confirmed the move, which was agreed to earlier this year in a peace deal with Israel.

items.title: Nigeria says two nationals killed in anti-migrant violence in South Africa
items.description: It said one of the men was allegedly killed by police officers in Pretoria and the other by unidentified attackers in Mpumalanga province.

items.title: Hamas dissolves civilian governing body in Gaza after 20 years
items.description: Hamas has announced the dissolution of its civilian governing body in Gaza after nearly 20 years of administration.
